name: Time Criticality (TC)

definition.summary: Time Criticality measures how the value of the work decays over time and the cost of delaying delivery.
details: Represents urgency based on deadlines, market windows, or escalating costs of delay
Assumes the business value is otherwise unchanged
characteristics: Independent of effort and job size
Distinct from overall business value magnitude
Driven by external or internal timing constraints

scoringPrinciples: Score based on how quickly value decays if delayed
Do not inflate scores due to personal preference or convenience
Deadlines should be explicit when possible
Anything above a score of 6 requires a clear timing rationale
Scores of 9–10 should be rare and well-justified

scaleDefinitions.label: No Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: No meaningful cost of delay. Timing does not impact value.
examples: Work that can be done at any time with no downside

scaleDefinitions.label: Very Low Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Minimal cost of delay. Long deferral would have little to no business impact.

scaleDefinitions.label: Low Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Some cost of delay over a long period, but no clear deadline or window.
examples: Incremental improvement with slow value decay

scaleDefinitions.label: Moderate-Low Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Value degrades slowly if delayed. Better sooner, but not time-sensitive.

scaleDefinitions.label: Moderate Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Noticeable cost of delay over time. Delaying too long creates lost opportunity.
examples: Competitive improvement with no hard deadline

scaleDefinitions.label: Moderate-High Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Clear and increasing cost of delay. Timing materially affects outcomes.
examples: Market opportunity with soft timing constraints

scaleDefinitions.label: High Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Strong time sensitivity. Delays will result in significant value loss.

scaleDefinitions.label: Very High Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Critical timing window. Missing the window causes substantial loss of value.

scaleDefinitions.label: Immediate Urgency
scaleDefinitions.description: Severe and imminent cost of delay. Each increment of time materially worsens outcomes.

scaleDefinitions.label: Emergency / Drop Everything
scaleDefinitions.description: Extreme urgency. Delay causes immediate and unacceptable business harm.
examples: Production outage or data loss risk

governanceGuidance: Require explicit timing rationale for Time Criticality scores of 7 or higher
Re-score Time Criticality when deadlines or conditions change
Audit for chronic overuse of high urgency scores
Treat Time Criticality as a decay function, not a proxy for importance
